target.name: Solinftec
target.founder: Britaldo Hernández Fernández, Enrique Ponce Caballero, Anselmo Del Toro Arce, Leslie González, Genrry Perez, Lazaro Victor Quintana Garcia, George Victor Diaz Calderin

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $40M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $1.8B.

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: The Bot Company
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B)

data.narrative:
Solinftec is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $40M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B Series B), Dexterity ($1.6B Series B), Hadrian ($1.6B Series C), and Nimble Robotics ($1.0B Series C)—supplemented by a broader universe of eight private robotics firms and one public comparable.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. The stage-progression method, applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $40M raised proxy, yields $76M. The peer-median method uses the four-company comparable set, which has a median valuation of $1.6B and a range of $1.0B to $1.8B. The peer cohort reflects material heterogeneity across stage and valuation, with Series B peers clustered near $1.6B–$1.8B while Series C peers span $1.0B–$1.6B.

Triangulation of these two methods produces a valuation range of $65M (low) to $1.9B (high), with a median of $850M. The wide range reflects tension between stage-progression mechanics—which emphasize early fundraising data—and peer comparables, which embed market prices for later-stage robotics companies. Key uncertainties include revenue traction relative to peers, unit economics, market position in specific robotics verticals, and whether Solinftec's development trajectory aligns with the higher-valuation subset of the peer set or the lower end.
